title: Postal-code (pincode) collection (per state)
description: All postal codes (India Post 6-digit pincodes) within a single state, keyed by the pincode itself. Analogous to subdistrict.schema.json but the issuing authority is India Post rather than LGD, so the pincode IS the canonical identifier (CLAUDE.md §3 — never invent IDs when an authority publishes one). One file per state at datasets/reference/in/states/<S>/postal.json. Joined to datasets/boundaries/in/postal/IN-pincodes-<city>.geojson via the `pincode` feature property. Per TODO/TN-GRANULAR-GEO-PLAN.md Phase 4 §160. v1.0 ships the schema only — the registry data file and the Chennai pincode polygon file are deferred behind the Phase 3 search affordance that will consume them; structural surface lands now so the loader's GeoLevel union and the schema-validator both know about `postal` before the data does. Pincode is a search-only layer (Jony edit §d): typed pincode → zoom to its polygon when present, otherwise fall back to district. Never a clickable choropleth.

x-changelog.description: Initial schema. Per-state postal registry mirroring subdistrict.schema.json shape, with two semantic differences: (1) `id` is the 6-digit pincode itself (id_source fixed to "indiapost"); (2) `district_id` is OPTIONAL because pincodes do not always nest cleanly inside a single revenue district (some span borders) — the field carries the predominant district where one exists. Required fields: $schema, $schema_version, sources, state, postal_codes (each with id, id_source, name). Optional per-item: name_ta, name_alt, district_id, subdistrict_id, headquarters, notes. Created in the same arc that lands the GeoLevel="postal" loader extension; the actual data files are not yet emitted (Fowler YAGNI — consumer is Phase 3 search affordance).

sources.description: Provenance per CLAUDE.md §12 / ADR-0002. Each entry is one upstream URL plus the timestamp our pipeline fetched it. An empty array signals hand-authored content.

state.description: ECI state code that this collection belongs to.

id.description: India Post 6-digit pincode. Leading zero is invalid (no Indian pincode begins with 0). Join key against `IN-pincodes-<city>.geojson` feature property `pincode`.

id_source.description: Fixed: India Post is the sole issuing authority for pincodes. Enum is preserved (rather than collapsing the field) for shape-symmetry with district / subdistrict registry items.

name.description: Post Office name as published by India Post (e.g. "T. Nagar S.O.").

name_ta.description: Optional Tamil-script localised post-office name. Tooltip / search affordance only — never an identifier.

name_alt.description: Optional citizen-recognised name where it diverges from the India Post canonical name.

district_id.description: OPTIONAL parent district id (resolves into the same state's districts.json under id_source=lgd). Optional because some pincodes span district borders — when set, this carries the PREDOMINANT district. Absence is a valid signal, not a data gap.

subdistrict_id.description: OPTIONAL parent subdistrict id (same resolution rules as district_id). Same predominant-not-exclusive caveat applies.
